LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HERTZ EQUIPMENT RENTAL CORPORATION, THE HERTZ CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - CONTINUED TO MAY 11, 2011 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-13-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HERTZ EQUIPMENT RENTAL CORPORATION AND THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S FEHA CLAIMS (1ST, 3RD, 5-6TH CAUSES OF ACTION) ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PLAINTIFF'S INITIAL DFEH COMPLAINT WAS FILED, AND THE "RIGHT TO SUE" LETTER ISSUED OVER ONE YEAR PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THIS ACTION. THE ADDITION OF INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT, BRUCE WALKER, DOES NOT TOLL THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BECAUSE HE TOO WAS MENTIONED SPECIFICALLY IN THE DFEH COMPLAINT. SAAVEDRA V. ORANGE COUNTY CONSOLIDATION TRANSP. SER. AGENCY (1992) 11 CAL.APP.4TH 824, 826-28. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO CONVINCING ARGUMENT WHY THE DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE TOLLING SHOULD APPLY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Defendant Bruce Walker, Hertz Equipment Rental Corporation, The Hertz Corporation Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on defendants Bruce Walker, Hertz Equipment Rental Corporation and the Hertz Corporations's Motion for Summary Judgment.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Meghan Palma, #13601.

Mark Hostetter appeared for plaintiff John Cambra, who was not present at today's hearing.
David A. Kolek and Robert A. Dolinko appeared for defendants Bruce Walker, Hertz Equipment Rental Corporation and the Hertz Corporation.

After hearing oral argument by counsel, the matter is SUBMITTED. The Court shall issue an Order After Hearing.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2011 Text Ruling

No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion will be heard by Judge James McBride in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m. on January 5, 2012. No court reporter will be provided. If a party desires to bring a court reporter, the parties need to meet and confer to agree on a single reporter. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS HERTZ EQUIPMENT RENTAL CORPORATION AND THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES.

APPEARANCES: Mark W. Hostetter, Esq. 408/375-8545 for pltf & opposing party; Marjorie S. Fochtman, Esq. of Nixon Peabody LLP 984-8443 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S FEHA CLAIMS (1ST, 3RD, 5-6TH CAUSES OF ACTION) ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PLAINTIFF'S INITIAL DFEH COMPLAINT WAS FILED, AND THE "RIGHT TO SUE" LETTER ISSUED OVER ONE YEAR PRIOR TO THE FILING OF THIS ACTION. THE ADDITION OF INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT, BRUCE WALKER, DOES NOT TOLL THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BECAUSE HE TOO WAS MENTIONED SPECIFICALLY IN THE DFEH COMPLAINT. SAAVEDRA V. ORANGE COUNTY CONSOLIDATION TRANSP. SER. AGENCY (1992) 11 CAL.APP.4TH 824, 826-28. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO CONVINCING ARGUMENT WHY THE DOCTRINE OF EQUITABLE TOLLING SHOULD APPLY. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2011 Continued To May-11-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT HERTZ EQUIPMENT RENTAL CORPORATION, THE HERTZ CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - CONTINUED TO MAY 11, 2011 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
